description_long: This object is a vintage-style jukebox vending machine with a prominent rectangular body and a curved top. The front features a large glass display area showcasing a record player mechanism and a grid of song selection cards, framed by metallic and teal accents. The lower section is covered with a perforated speaker grille, and the sides are decorated with a teal pattern and metallic trim. The back is solid and unadorned, with a simple black finish. The machine has visible buttons and a coin slot on the front, typical of classic jukeboxes, and the overall construction appears robust and heavy, likely made from metal and glass. The design is both decorative and functional, evoking mid-20th-century Americana.
description: A vintage jukebox vending machine with a glass display, metallic trim, teal accents, and a prominent speaker grille.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass display with record player, song selection cards, buttons, and speaker grille.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the teal patterned panel and the depth of the machine.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, solid black back panel with minimal detailing.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the teal patterned panel and metallic trim.
attribute_display_panel: A large glass display panel on the upper front, rectangular in shape, showing song selection cards and the record player mechanism.
attribute_payment_interface: A coin slot is visible on the front, above the speaker grille, typical of classic jukeboxes.
attribute_product_selection_buttons: Multiple individual selection buttons are visible below the song cards, arranged in a horizontal row.
attribute_product_retrieval_slot: A retrieval slot is located at the lower front, integrated into the speaker grille area.
attribute_branding_or_signage: Red 'AMi' branding is visible at the top center of the front panel, with additional small logos on the lower grille.
